varieties.name: Magnaye
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 1999 Pl-02)
varieties.traits: Relatively short (7.5m after 16 years), spreading canopy, high-yielding variety
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, year-round bearing. Highest anthocyanin content (305mg/100g) in pulp
varieties.growing_notes: BEST FOR Humid subtropical: Compact size ideal for suburban gardens. Heat-tolerant. Requires well-drained soil and frost protection in winter. Grafted trees bear in 3-4 years. Superior butyric acid production for gut health
varieties.availability: Limited availability in Australia - may require importation of grafted material from Philippines nurseries or Hawaii research stations

varieties.name: Magayon
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 2000 Pl-05) - Named after legendary Princess Magayon
varieties.traits: Tall (8m after 19 years), spreading canopy, easy to propagate asexually
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, year-round bearing, tolerant to pests and diseases

varieties.name: Laysa
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 1999 Pl-03)
varieties.traits: Tall (10m after 16 years), spreading canopy, high-yielding, easy asexual propagation
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, year-round bearing, excellent pest/disease tolerance

varieties.name: Lanuza
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 2000 Pl-04)
varieties.traits: Medium height, spreading canopy, good propagation success
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable producer with consistent annual yields

varieties.name: M. Orolfo
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 1999 Pl-01)
varieties.traits: Medium-tall growth habit, moderate canopy spread
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity with year-round fruiting potential

varieties.name: Mayon #1
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 2003 Pl-06) - Named after Mt. Mayon volcano
varieties.traits: Vigorous growth, moderate height, adapted to volcanic soils
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable annual production, adapts well to various soil types
varieties.growing_notes: Volcanic soil preference aligns with Humid subtropical basalt-derived soils in some areas. Heat and humidity tolerant. Requires frost-free location. Symbolically connected to Bicol's volcanic heritage
varieties.availability: Research stations and specialty nurseries; limited commercial availability in Australia

varieties.name: Orbase
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 2012 Pl-12) - Most recent certified variety
varieties.traits: Modern improved selection, optimized growth characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Enhanced productivity and consistency

varieties.name: Poamoho
varieties.origin: Hawaii, USA (USDA-ARS National Clonal Germplasm Repository release)
varieties.traits: Developed for subtropical/tropical cultivation outside Philippines. Improved propagation characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Good production with superior quality attributes

varieties.name: Philippine Wild Type
varieties.origin: Bicol Region wild stands, Philippines (Heritage trees, 100+ years old in some areas)
varieties.traits: Highly variable - tall trees 20-25m, broad genetic diversity, extremely wind-resistant
varieties.yield_notes: Variable 50-200kg in-shell nuts per mature tree. Harvest May-October
varieties.growing_notes: HERITAGE VALUE for Humid subtropical: Represents pure genetic stock from native Philippine forests. Seedling trees do NOT come true to type - each is unique. Superior windbreak for subtropical storms. May take 6-7 years to fruit from seed. Important for genetic diversity conservation. Wild collection supports Philippine indigenous communities
varieties.availability: Seeds occasionally available from Philippines specialty suppliers; wild-collected material requires phytosanitary certification for Australia

varieties.name: Katutubo
varieties.origin: Philippines indigenous selection (Name means "native" or "indigenous person" in Tagalog)
varieties.traits: Traditional landrace variety, adapted to harsh conditions, variable growth
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good production depending on tree

varieties.name: Red Variety
varieties.origin: Philippines (early selection, pre-NSIC certification era)
varieties.traits: Distinctive reddish tinge to new growth or bark, medium-tall tree
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable production, adapted to Philippine tropical conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Early improved selection. May show better cold tolerance due to highland adaptation. Suitable for humid subtropical climates trial plantings in warm microclimates. Genetic preservation value
varieties.availability: Rare in cultivation; Philippine agricultural universities and germplasm collections

varieties.name: Albay Landrace
varieties.origin: Albay Province, Philippines - center of pili genetic diversity
varieties.traits: Regional ecotype adapted to volcanic slopes and typhoon conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Proven productivity in native range, harvest traditions maintained by local communities
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS SIGNIFICANCE: Represents centuries of natural and farmer selection in primary pili-growing region. Volcanic soil adaptation may suit Humid subtropical basalt soils. Genetic diversity highest of all types. Cultural connection to Mt. Mayon region. Seedlings preserve landracecharacteristics
varieties.availability: Seed collection from Albay heritage trees; Albay Research and Development Center; requires importation procedures

varieties.name: Magnaye
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 1999 Pl-02)
varieties.traits: Compact (7.5m after 16 years), spreading canopy, highest-yielding variety
varieties.yield_notes: EXCEPTIONAL productivity, continuous year-round bearing. Highest anthocyanin antioxidants
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR tropical monsoon climates: Thrives in tropical heat and monsoon humidity. Compact size ideal for Tropical monsoon dense cultivation areas. Perfect climate match - no cold stress. Year-round fruiting aligns with Tropical monsoon harvest patterns. Highest nutritional value. Ideal for commercial production and home gardens
varieties.availability: Philippine nurseries (Cainta Plant Nursery, Albay Research stations); Import via tropical monsoon climates agricultural departments; Tissue culture labs in tropical monsoon climates could propagate

varieties.name: Magayon
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 2000 Pl-05) - Named after Princess Magayon of Mt. Mayon legend
varieties.traits: Medium height (8m after 19 years), spreading canopy, easy propagation
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity, year-round fruiting, pest/disease tolerant
varieties.growing_notes: IDEAL FOR tropical monsoon climates MONSOON: Handles heavy rainfall exceptionally well. Cultural significance may appeal to Tropical monsoon story-rich traditions. Proven tropical performance. Easy establishment from grafted material. Suitable for intercropping with coconut, areca nut
varieties.availability: Philippine agricultural research stations; tropical monsoon climates horticultural societies could facilitate import; potential for local propagation programs

varieties.name: Laysa
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 1999 Pl-03)
varieties.traits: Tall (10m after 16 years), spreading canopy, high-yielding
varieties.yield_notes: High continuous production, excellent pest/disease resistance
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT FOR Tropical monsoon TROPICAL CLIMATE: Thrives in heat and humidity. Superior fiber content valuable for health food markets. Pest resistance important in monsoon humidity. Tall stature suitable for Tropical monsoon agroforestry systems. Year-round bearing matches Tropical monsoon continuous harvest season
varieties.availability: Philippine Department of Agriculture research stations; tropical monsoon climates agricultural universities (KAU) could coordinate import; grafting trials possible in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Lanuza
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 2000 Pl-04)
varieties.traits: Medium height, spreading canopy, reliable growth
varieties.yield_notes: Consistent production, reliable annual yields
varieties.growing_notes: Well-adapted to monsoon conditions. Moderate size suits tropical monsoon climates gardens. Proven tropical performance. Good choice for mixed plantations with traditional tropical monsoon climates crops (coconut, jackfruit, mango)

varieties.name: M. Orolfo
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 1999 Pl-01)
varieties.traits: Medium-tall, good canopy development
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, year-round potential

varieties.name: Mayon #1
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 2003 Pl-06) - Named after Mt. Mayon
varieties.traits: Vigorous growth, adapted to volcanic soils, moderate height
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable tropical production, adapts to various soils

varieties.name: Orbase
varieties.origin: Albay, Philippines (NSIC 2012 Pl-12) - Latest certified variety
varieties.traits: Modern selection with optimized characteristics, improved disease resistance
varieties.yield_notes: Improved productivity and consistency over older varieties

varieties.name: Poamoho
varieties.origin: Hawaii, USA (USDA-ARS release) - Subtropical/tropical adapted
varieties.traits: Bred for cultivation outside native range, improved propagation
varieties.yield_notes: Good production, superior processing quality

varieties.name: Philippine Wild Type
varieties.origin: Bicol Region natural forests, Philippines (Ancient heritage trees, some 150+ years old)
varieties.traits: MAXIMUM genetic diversity. Tall 20-25m, extreme wind resistance, adapted to typhoons and monsoons
varieties.yield_notes: Mature trees 50-200kg in-shell nuts. Proven monsoon performance. Extended harvest season May-October
varieties.growing_notes: CRITICAL FOR tropical monsoon climates BIODIVERSITY: Pure genetic stock from native range. Essential for establishing genetic diversity in tropical monsoon climates cultivation. Climate match with tropical monsoon climates near-perfect. Seedlings create varied population - select best performers for future propagation. Supports Philippine indigenous forest communities. Monsoon-proven genetics. Important for long-term sustainability of tropical monsoon climates pili industry
varieties.availability: Wild-collected seeds from Philippine indigenous communities; requires proper collection permits and phytosanitary certification; tropical monsoon climates Forest Department coordination

varieties.name: Katutubo (Native)
varieties.origin: Philippines - Indigenous landrace (Name: "indigenous/native person" in Tagalog)
varieties.traits: Traditional variety predating modern breeding. Hardy, monsoon-adapted, variable growth patterns
varieties.yield_notes: Proven productivity in harsh tropical conditions. Centuries of natural selection

varieties.name: Albay Volcanic Landrace
varieties.origin: Albay Province, Philippines - Mt. Mayon region (PRIMARY genetic diversity center)
varieties.traits: Regional ecotype - volcanic soil specialist, typhoon-resistant, monsoon-adapted. Variable trees reflecting centuries of selection
varieties.yield_notes: Proven production in extreme conditions (volcanic eruptions, typhoons, heavy monsoons)
varieties.growing_notes: MOST IMPORTANT FOR tropical monsoon climates: Center of pili genetic diversity - highest genetic variation. Volcanic soil adaptation valuable for Tropical monsoon laterite soils. EXTREME monsoon tolerance (Albay receives 3000-4000mm rainfall like tropical monsoon climates). Cultural significance - heart of pili nut tradition. Seeds from this landrace create population with maximum adaptability. Essential genetic resource for establishing tropical monsoon climates pili cultivation
varieties.availability: Albay Research and Development Center; Bicol indigenous farmers; Mt. Mayon region heritage tree seeds; requires coordination with Philippine Department of Agriculture and tropical monsoon climates State authorities

varieties.name: Red Variety
varieties.origin: Philippines - Early selection, pre-certification era
varieties.traits: Distinctive reddish coloration in new growth, medium-tall stature
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable tropical production
varieties.growing_notes: Early improved selection with proven tropical performance. Genetic diversity value. Potentially unique biochemical characteristics (red pigmentation). Suitable for tropical monsoon climates research programs studying pili adaptation and nutrition
varieties.availability: Philippine germplasm collections; agricultural university research stations; rare in commercial cultivation

varieties.name: Sorsogon Landrace
varieties.origin: Sorsogon Province, Philippines - Southern Luzon wild stands
varieties.traits: Coastal-adapted ecotype, salt spray tolerant, extreme wind resistance
varieties.yield_notes: Proven coastal tropical production
varieties.growing_notes: VALUABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates COASTAL AREAS: Coastal adaptation suits Tropical monsoon extensive coastline. Salt spray tolerance for near-coastal planting. Wind resistance for monsoon storms. Genetic diversity different from Albay types. Represents southern distribution of pili in Philippines. Potential for tropical monsoon climates coastal agroforestry
varieties.availability: Sorsogon wild collection; coastal community seed networks; Philippine coastal forestry programs

varieties.name: Camarines Sur Heritage Trees
varieties.origin: Camarines Sur Province, Philippines - Eastern Luzon traditional groves
varieties.traits: Long-established landrace selections, proven genetic base
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable monsoon-zone production

varieties.name: Canarium indicum (Galip Nut, Nangai Nut)
varieties.origin: Papua New Guinea, Solomon Islands, Vanuatu, Northern Australia
varieties.traits: Large tree 30m+, buttressed trunk 1m+ diameter, similar to pili

varieties.name: Canarium album (Chinese White Olive)
varieties.origin: China - 2000+ year cultivation history
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree, tolerates light frost (MORE cold-hardy than pili). Subtropical-tropical range

varieties.name: Canarium vulgare (Java Almond, Kenari)
varieties.origin: Indonesia, Malaysia - Maritime Southeast Asia
varieties.traits: Similar to C. ovatum but distinct. Tropical lowland tree
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates: True tropical species. Perfect for monsoon climate. Commercial use in Indonesia. Processing traditions available. Cultural connection to Southeast Asian diaspora in tropical monsoon climates
varieties.availability: Indonesian/Malaysian nurseries; regional seed networks; requires import certification

varieties.name: Canarium luzonicum (Manila Elemi)
varieties.origin: Philippines (Quezon Province) - Indigenous
varieties.traits: Tall tree 35m, diameter 1m. Primarily grown for ELEMI RESIN (perfume, medicine)
varieties.growing_notes: DUAL-PURPOSE CROP: Elemi resin highly valuable (perfumery, aromatherapy, traditional medicine). Nuts edible but resin primary product. Indigenous Philippine species. Cultural significance. Potential alternative crop for Tropical monsoon spice/medicinal plant industry. Research collaboration opportunity
varieties.availability: Philippine forestry programs; indigenous resin collectors; limited cultivation - mostly wild harvest
